overview: A three-tier leave management system consisting of a React frontend web application, a Go-based REST API backend service, and a PostgreSQL database. The frontend communicates with the backend via HTTP REST calls, the backend enforces business logic and manages leave requests, approvals, and balances, and all data persists in PostgreSQL.
requirements: Employees must be able to submit leave requests by selecting start/end dates and leave type
Managers must see pending leave requests from their direct reports
Managers must be able to approve or reject leave requests with optional comments
Employees must be able to view current leave balances by leave type
All users must be able to view their own request history and approval status
System must store leave types, balances, requests, and approval workflows
Backend must expose health check endpoint at /health
Frontend must be a single-page application accessible via browser

components.name: leave-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Management API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: REST API for managing employee leave requests, approvals, and balances
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /leave-requests:
    get:
      summary: List leave requests
      operationId: listLeaveRequests
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: managerId
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of leave requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
    post:
      summary: Create a leave request
      operationId: createLeaveRequest
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/CreateLeaveRequestInput'
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Leave request created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid input
  /leave-requests/{id}:
    get:
      summary: Get a leave request by ID
      operationId: getLeaveRequest
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave request details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '404':
          description: Not found
  /leave-requests/{id}/approve:
    post:
      summary: Approve a leave request
      operationId: approveLeaveRequest
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                managerId:
                  type: string
                comment:
                  type: string
              required:
                - managerId
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request approved
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '404':
          description: Not found
  /leave-requests/{id}/reject:
    post:
      summary: Reject a leave request
      operationId: rejectLeaveRequest
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                managerId:
                  type: string
                comment:
                  type: string
              required:
                - managerId
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request rejected
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '404':
          description: Not found
  /leave-balances:
    get:
      summary: Get leave balances for an employee
      operationId: getLeaveBalances
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave balances by type
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveBalance'
  /leave-types:
    get:
      summary: List available leave types
      operationId: listLeaveTypes
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave types
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveType'
components:
  schemas:
    LeaveRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        employeeId:
          type: string
        employeeName:
          type: string
        managerId:
          type: string
        leaveTypeId:
          type: string
        leaveTypeName:
          type: string
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
        comment:
          type: string
        createdAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updatedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
    CreateLeaveRequestInput:
      type: object
      properties:
        employeeId:
          type: string
        employeeName:
          type: string
        managerId:
          type: string
        leaveTypeId:
          type: string
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
      required:
        - employeeId
        - employeeName
        - managerId
        - leaveTypeId
        - startDate
        - endDate
    LeaveBalance:
      type: object
      properties:
        employeeId:
          type: string
        leaveTypeId:
          type: string
        leaveTypeName:
          type: string
        balance:
          type: number
          format: float
    LeaveType:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        name:
          type: string
        defaultBalance:
          type: number
          format: float
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go-based REST API service using net/http on port 9090. Use PostgreSQL as the database (connection string from environment variable DATABASE_URL). Create tables: leave_types (id, name, default_balance), leave_balances (employee_id, leave_type_id, balance), leave_requests (id, employee_id, employee_name, manager_id, leave_type_id, start_date, end_date, status [pending/approved/rejected], comment, created_at, updated_at). Seed initial leave types: Annual (20 days), Sick (10 days), Casual (5 days). Implement all OpenAPI endpoints. GET /leave-requests supports filtering by employeeId, managerId, and status query parameters. POST /leave-requests validates date ranges (endDate >= startDate), checks sufficient leave balance, creates request with status=pending, deducts balance only when approved. Approve and reject endpoints update status, set comment, record updatedAt. GET /leave-balances returns all leave type balances for an employeeId. Handle errors gracefully with appropriate HTTP status codes. Use standard library database/sql with a PostgreSQL driver. Include Dockerfile with multi-stage build. Expose /health endpoint returning {"status":"ok"}. Use environment variables for configuration. Log all requests and errors to stdout.

components.name: leave-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Management Web Application
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Frontend single-page application for leave management
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:3000
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Application is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a React single-page application using TypeScript, Vite, and React Router. Use a component library like Material-UI or Ant Design for UI elements. The app runs on port 3000 and proxies API requests to the leave-api service (configure via environment variable REACT_APP_API_URL defaulting to http://localhost:9090). Create pages: (1) Employee Dashboard — form to submit leave requests (date pickers for start/end, dropdown for leave type), display current leave balances in cards, list of user's own leave requests with status badges. (2) Manager Dashboard — table of pending leave requests from direct reports, each row has Approve and Reject buttons with optional comment input. (3) Request History — filterable table showing all requests with status, dates, leave type, and manager comments. Implement client-side routing. Use React hooks and context for state management. Fetch leave types on mount. Validate form inputs (end date >= start date, required fields). Show loading spinners during API calls. Display error messages for failed requests. Use responsive design for mobile and desktop. Include a navigation bar with links to Employee and Manager dashboards. Implement a simple mock authentication context that stores employeeId and managerId in local storage (hardcode a few test users for demo purposes). Dockerfile should use multi-stage build: build with node:18, serve with nginx, copy build output to /usr/share/nginx/html. Expose /health endpoint via nginx returning {"status":"ok"}. Configure nginx to serve index.html for all routes (SPA fallback).
